## 5. Feature coverage

- THING routing: [x] answer [x] research [x] build space [x] app-4a (automator) [ ] app-4b
  (build_app) [ ] code (engineer) [x] memory [x] install+automate [x] compound request
  [x] provided-info shortcut [x] restraint/refusal [x] multilingual
- Spaces: [x] create per-part [x] live-registered/delegatable [ ] no-clobber re-add
- Event pipeline: [x] webhook [x] cron [ ] db [ ] internal · [ ] code-handler hook [x] agent-trigger
  hook · [ ] code nodes [x] forEach · [ ] project functions · [ ] loop guard [ ] payload validation
  [ ] emitEvent
- Consent/caps: [ ] @consent [x] installSpace approve/deny (approve only) [ ] fail-closed headless
  [ ] capability gating
- Store/integrations: [x] discovery (`storeSearch`/`storeInspect`) [x] install a space
  [x] callConnection [x] inbound webhook [x] integration-demo source
- Project-app: [x] writeProjectTable(+rows seed) [x] writeProjectPage/Api [x] db:write later-update
  [x] app build [x] /app/<id>/ serving [x] app data API
- Attachments: [x] upload [x] readDocument [x] attachmentIds to a specialist [x] vision/audio
- Pod lifecycle: [x] restart→auto-resume [ ] cold-wake [ ] event storm [ ] worker containment
- Cross-cutting: [x] edge cases/errors [x] performance [ ] budget

---

## 6. Acceptance criteria (the Acts)

The runner (`05-latam/run.mjs`) drives these and asserts on the **trace + real pod state**.

| Act | Asserts (trace + real state) | Stories |
|---|---|---|
| **I — The dump & the unprompted offer** | `trip-notes.md` uploaded via `sendWithAttachments`; a `display` event containing an offer (turning it into "something you can open") appears **before** her "yes please" turn; `pod.listSpaces`/project manifest shows **no `database/`** yet (no over-scaffolding on a vague opener) | US-1 |
| **II — Invisible research + the entry-requirements space + the PDF's fact** | The compound turn (border admin + price-watch) produces **two** distinct traces of work: `didDelegate('system-research')` with ≥1 real `webSearch`/`webFetch` yield citing at least one of the `links.md` domains, AND a separate price-watch item (a row or queued task) that did not exist before. A space with `knowledge/` exists on disk (`pod.listSpaces`); a knowledge file under it contains the exact string `Huchuypicchu`; the agent's frontmatter declares at least one 2-part (`domain/field`) on-demand ref and one 3-part (`domain/field/option`) preloaded ref | US-2 |
| **III — `loadKnowledge`: on-demand vs preloaded, proven** | A Brazil-e-visa-specific follow-up produces a `{type:'yield', kind:'loadKnowledge'}` trace event that turn; a Machu-Picchu-circuit-specific follow-up is answered correctly (names the high-season-only `Huchuypicchu` route) with **zero** `loadKnowledge` yields that turn — the fact was already in the system prompt | US-3 |
| **IV — Attachments feed the app (tokens land, not prose)** | `salar-de-uyuni…jpg` and `trip-budget.xlsx` uploaded with short captions. `pod.appBuild` succeeds; `pod.appData(id,'itinerary')` has ≥15 rows matching the xlsx (incl. a Bolivia/Sucre row with **`nights` null**, annotated TBD); `pod.appData(id,'budget')` has a row carrying `Torres del Paine`; `pod.appData(id,'highlights')` (or equivalent) has a row whose `filename`/reference contains `2016-02-04`; `pod.appData(id,'stays')` has a La Paz row carrying `Wild Rover`. **Anti-expectation:** after the xlsx ingest, no *new* `webSearch` yield re-derives a cost the spreadsheet already gave (provided-info shortcut) | US-2 (cont.), US-5 |
| **V — The app renders, and evolves itself from inside** | Chrome-devtools: session injected on both the pod and served-app origins; `pod.appPage(id,'')` renders the built bundle; the rendered DOM shows the real `itinerary`/`budget` values (non-zero, actual fixture numbers), the in-app chat panel is present, **no console errors, no failed fetches**; `pod.appApi(id, <a real route the page fetches>)` returns 200 with the right shape (not just the raw data API). **Then**, through that same in-app chat, she asks in plain words for a new tracking spot (e.g. "who to text when I land") — a **new table/page** exists afterward that did not exist before this turn | US-5, US-6 |
| **VI — Restraint: "book that flight for me"** | Her LA2232 ask gets **no** booking-confirmed write to `itinerary`/any bookings table, **no** payment/booking `Form` ask raised; the reply states the limitation (tolerate curly apostrophes) | US-7 |
| **VII — Store discovery before install, then a real round-trip** | Trace order: `didYield('storeSearch')` (or equivalent) **before** `didYield('storeInspect')` **before** the `ConsentCard` ask; a `display` event with a plain-words explanation of the option precedes that ask; her "yes okay" approves it; `installSpace` yield follows; `pod.listIntegrations(id)` includes it. A signed `pod.inbound('demo', body, {sig})` afterward returns `{events:1}`/200 and a reply reaches the project via `callConnection` | US-8 |
| **VIII — Cron with a `ctx.state` cursor** | `pod.runEmitter(id, scope, 'weekly-digest')` (or its authored name) forced twice back-to-back with no new underlying data between runs. Run 1's resulting row/message lists ≥1 item; run 2's resulting row lists **0** items already surfaced in run 1 (assert the *set* of ids, not text); `pod.readProjectFile(id, '.data/emitter-state.json')` (or the authored state key) shows the cursor value changed between the two runs | US-9 |
| **IX — Tasklist DAG: `forEach` × `dependsOn` × `condition` × `optional`** | The authored tasklist file declares: a `forEach` node fanning out over confirmed-route countries, a `dependsOn` edge, a `condition` that **skips Brazil** (still "later" per her own notes — assert a `node_end`/`skipped` trace entry naming it), and an `optional` node whose forced failure is **skipped**, not fatal (tasklist still completes, `ok:true`). The Bolivia branch's output/display references her sister's altitude story **unprompted** (memory recall, US-4) | US-4, US-10 |
| **X — Tasklist forced degraded** | The runner patches the SAME tasklist's goal node (via `pod.readProjectFile`+`pod.writeFile`) with one impossible required output field, re-fires it via the cron hook, and reads the `tasklist()` yield's resolved value off the trace: exactly `{ok:false, degraded:true, reason, degradedTasks:[<goalNodeId>]}` — not a thrown error, not a hang. The file is reverted afterward and a clean re-run returns `ok:true` again | US-10 |
| **XI — History summarization survives 20+ turns** | The turn-3 message states her `$9,000` ceiling. ≥17 further turns of real unrelated chatter follow. The recall turn's `lastText` states the correct figure. A subsequent `llm_request` trace event's `messages[0].content` starts with `[CONTEXT SUMMARY]` (summarization actually fired, not just a long session) | US-11 |
| **XII — Spanish: voice memo + a typed switch, both write real rows** | `voice-memo.mp3` uploaded via `sendWithAttachments`; the `itinerary` Bolivia/Sucre row's `nights` field goes from **null → 4** (the memo's actual change of mind), and a field on that row (or a linked note) carries `Churuquella`. Separately, her later plain-Spanish-typed message ("quita Buenos Aires… más días en El Calafate") results in the Buenos Aires `itinerary` row removed/marked skipped and the El Calafate row's `nights` increased — a real `db:write`, not a reply. A following **English** message routes correctly afterward (no degradation) | US-12 |
| **XIII — Pod restart → auto-resume mid-trip** | `pod.restart()`; the runner's `send()` sees the 404, waits for the pod, resumes the same session id; the reply continues the conversation coherently; a forced cron run (Act VIII's digest) still fires post-restart; all rows written before the restart are still present via `pod.appData` | US-13 |

*Performance targets are **hang detectors, not SLOs**. Record the ACTUAL time as a metric on every
Act; only FAIL when a ceiling below is breached — that means something is broken, not merely slow.*

### Performance targets
| Metric | Target |
|---|---|
| Attachment ingest → token in real state (per fixture: notes/PDF/xlsx/image/audio) | < 5 min |
| Space creation (entry-requirements) | < 10 min |
| App build (`POST …/app/build`) | < 90 s |
| Served app first byte | < 5 s |
| Store discovery → consent → approved | < 2 min |
| Forced cron digest run | < 5 min |
| Weekly tasklist run (happy path, ~5 countries fanned out) | < 45 min |
| Forced-degraded tasklist run | < 5 min |
| Whole scenario wall clock | ≤ 4 h |
| Eval/typecheck errors (unrecovered) | **0** (hard fail) |

---

## 7. What this scenario is really testing (and the gap it closes)

Every other scenario has already proven that a project can grow from one conversation into a real
app. This one exists to drive **six specific runtime capabilities that no scenario has ever
exercised live**: a tasklist's full DAG surface (`forEach`, `dependsOn`, `condition`, `optional`)
including its **degraded** (not thrown, not hung) failure mode; `storeSearch`/`storeInspect` as
genuine pre-consent *browsing*, not a shortcut straight to a `ConsentCard`; a cron emitter's
`ctx.state` actually persisting a cursor across independent invocations (not just in-process
memory); the practical difference between a **preloaded** and an **on-demand** `loadKnowledge` ref
and whether a test can actually observe which one fired; whether history summarization genuinely
survives a long, messy, human conversation without losing an early stated fact; and whether Spanish
input is treated as a first-class write path, not a special case. If any of the six turns out to be
unreliable against a real model in production, that is the honest finding this scenario exists to
surface — not a reason to soften the assertion.

## 8. Running it

```bash
cd sdk/org/scenarios/harness
node smoke.mjs                       # prove harness + prod healthy first
node ../05-latam/run.mjs             # fresh; writes 05-latam/results/report.md
node ../05-latam/run.mjs --reuse     # reuse the cached user + project
node ../05-latam/run.mjs --acts=9,10 # rerun just the tasklist Acts while triaging
```

## Actual results

### Round 1 — baseline · 2026-07-14 · verdict: **FAIL (Act I)** — honest, and the finding is the point

`run.mjs` is implemented 1:1 with the Acts table (I–XIII). Act I ran live against prod repeatedly;
**Acts II–XIII are not yet run** (the run is checkpointed and resumable via `--acts=`).

**Act I FAILS, and it fails on the scenario's central promise (US-1).** Elena's opener — notes
attached, *"help me actually get on top of this"* — does not reliably produce an **offer**.

| # | Finding | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **THING never OFFERED** (deployed image) | In the **13,079 chars she actually sees**: zero occurrences of *"want me to / shall I / should I / would you like / I can build / turn this into"*, zero mentions of *open / dashboard / one place*. The only 3 `?` are quotes from her own notes. She then says "yes please" — *yes to what?* — and THING builds the whole app anyway, **on a consent it never asked for**. |
| 2 | Root cause: a **prompt** bug, fixed in source by the concurrent 06-tanzania agent (`11a9396`, "PROPOSE unasked"), **not deployed** | The live image's `instruct.md` is 27,067b and contains **no offer rule at all**. |
| 3 | That fix **live-verified by hot-patch** | `PUT /api/fs/write` → `system/spaces/user-thing/agents/thing/instruct.md` + restart ⇒ the offer appears, with **0 authoring yields** (restraint holds: offer first, build only on consent). |
| 4 | **But the offer is NONDETERMINISTIC** (~5/6) | Measured over **15 live openers** with `harness/probe-ab.mjs`. Every single miss has the same shape: a **long** reply — misses at **5,071 / 6,611 / 4,715** visible chars; offers at 1.2k–3.4k. THING spends the turn proving it read the material and stops exactly where the help begins. |
| 5 | **Restraint REGRESSED on current HEAD** | On the newest instruct (`0a99b59`, `e1620bd`, `2d8e9fd`), the opener produced **no offer AND 6 database tables** (`bookings, budget_log, contacts, packing, stops, todos`) — it scaffolded unasked, **via the automator delegate**, so no `writeProject*` yield appeared in THING's own stream. The runner's restraint check was blind to this and now asserts on build **delegates** too. |
| 6 | 101 recovered typecheck errors in one session | 0 unrecovered (the hard check holds), but the authoring retry surface is noisy. |

**Prompt-fix attempts that did NOT work — reported, not hidden.** Two edits to THING's brain (a
"ground every cited specific in THEIR material" rule; an "your reply is not the summary — do not
bury the offer" rule) were A/B'd at N=6 and N=3 against HEAD: **5/6 → 6/6 and → 2/3, i.e. within
noise.** Neither is evidenced, so **both were reverted** rather than ship an unproven change to a
brain every scenario shares. The measured correlation (long reply ⇒ no offer) is robust; the cure
is not yet found. **An honest FAIL beats a fake PASS.**

**Harness bug fixed (affects ALL SIX scenarios).** A run died with an uncaught
`TypeError: fetch failed` (undici `ConnectTimeoutError` to `lmthing.chat:443`): `pod.req` retried
HTTP *statuses* (`{waking:true}`/504) but never a bare `fetch()` **throw**. Now retried at the
transport layer (`fetchResilient`); a non-transient error still throws at once and a real 500 still
passes through. The harness had **zero test coverage** (`scenarios/` was in no vitest include
pattern) — added, plus 8 unit tests. Fix: sdk/org `c924339`.

**Assertion bugs fixed in this runner (made stronger, never looser).** The offer check regexed the
raw JSX descriptor JSON (missing prose in `children`, matching structural keys nobody said) — it now
flattens to the text the user actually SEES, and additionally requires the offer to **ask** a
question she can answer with a bare "yes". Fix: sdk/org `9ad4ff8`.

286	   store/track/manage X") — that is path 4, NOT path 3.
287	
288	4. **Build an APPLICATION** — when the user EXPLICITLY asks for a working *app*: a UI they can
289	   open (pages/screens/"an app I can open on my phone"), a dashboard, and/or persistent data with
290	   web pages — e.g. "build me a personalized feed", "an app to track my workouts", "a reading list
291	   with a page to mark items read", "turn this into an app I can open".
292	
293	   **Do NOT scaffold an app on a vague or exploratory request.** Building an app is a large,
294	   expensive commitment — never the response to "start a project", "help me keep track of X",
295	   "set up a project for my trip", or any opening message that does not name a UI/pages/dashboard.
296	   For those, take path 1: converse, orient, and set the project up LIGHTLY (answer, capture what
297	   they told you). Grow the project incrementally (documents, then per-topic spaces via path 3)
298	   and only reach for path 4 LATER, when the user actually asks to turn it into an app. If in
299	   doubt, ask one short clarifying question instead of building — an unwanted 6-table app is a far
300	   worse failure than one extra question.
301	
302	   **But OFFER — do not wait to be asked.** Restraint is not silence. The user does not know an app
303	   is even on the menu, so they will never name one; they will just describe a mess and hand you
304	   their material. When the user gives you SUBSTANTIAL MATERIAL (documents, a spreadsheet, photos,
305	   a data dump) **and** describes an ONGOING need to keep track of it ("I keep losing this", "I
306	   can't stay on top of it", "I don't want to be digging through files when it matters"), that is
307	   your cue to **propose** — in one short, plain sentence, offering to put it somewhere they can
308	   open and check, naming the REAL specifics you just read so they can tell you actually read them.
309	   Then **STOP and wait**. Do not author anything on the same turn as the offer.
310	
311	   Their agreement is the explicit request path 4 requires — and it will be plain and unspecific
312	   ("yes please", "go on then", "sure"). A bare yes to YOUR OWN offer is CONSENT: take path 4a
313	   immediately and build what you proposed. Do not ask them to spec it out, and never make them ask
314	   twice — re-offering the thing they just accepted is the same failure as never offering it.
315	
316	   **When the material splits into distinct PARTS, build the spaces too — they will never ask for
317	   them.** Substantial material usually has natural parts (per place, per stage, per client, per
318	   piece of equipment). Give each part its own specialist space, grounded in the provided content
319	   (path 3's ALREADY-PROVIDED shortcut — the architect, no re-research), so a later plain question
320	   about that part is answered from real knowledge instead of a stale summary. This is YOUR
321	   judgment call, not a request to relay: the user does not know what a specialist is and must
322	   never be asked to name one. Build the parts first, then the app, and report both in the terms
323	   they used — the parts of their own material, never the machinery.
324	
325	   **Two app targets — pick by WHERE the app should live (this matters a lot):**

   expensive commitment — never the response to "start a project", "help me keep track of X",
   "set up a project for my trip", or any opening message that does not name a UI/pages/dashboard.
   For those, take path 1: converse, orient, and set the project up LIGHTLY (answer, capture what
   they told you). Grow the project incrementally (documents, then per-topic spaces via path 3)
   and only reach for path 4 LATER, when the user actually asks to turn it into an app. If in
   doubt, ask one short clarifying question instead of building — an unwanted 6-table app is a far
   worse failure than one extra question.

   **But OFFER — do not wait to be asked.** Restraint is not silence. The user does not know an app
   is even on the menu, so they will never name one; they will just describe a mess and hand you
   their material. When the user gives you SUBSTANTIAL MATERIAL (documents, a spreadsheet, photos,
   a data dump) **and** describes an ONGOING need to keep track of it ("I keep losing this", "I
   can't stay on top of it", "I don't want to be digging through files when it matters"), that is
   your cue to **propose** — in one short, plain sentence, offering to put it somewhere they can
   open and check, naming the REAL specifics you just read so they can tell you actually read them.
   Then **STOP and wait**. Do not author anything on the same turn as the offer.

   Their agreement is the explicit request path 4 requires — and it will be plain and unspecific
   ("yes please", "go on then", "sure"). A bare yes to YOUR OWN offer is CONSENT: take path 4a
   immediately and build what you proposed. Do not ask them to spec it out, and never make them ask
   twice — re-offering the thing they just accepted is the same failure as never offering it.

   **When the material splits into distinct PARTS, build the spaces too — they will never ask for
   them.** Substantial material usually has natural parts (per place, per stage, per client, per
   piece of equipment). Give each part its own specialist space, grounded in the provided content
   (path 3's ALREADY-PROVIDED shortcut — the architect, no re-research), so a later plain question
   about that part is answered from real knowledge instead of a stale summary. This is YOUR
   judgment call, not a request to relay: the user does not know what a specialist is and must
   never be asked to name one. Build the parts first, then the app, and report both in the terms
   they used — the parts of their own material, never the machinery.

   **Two app targets — pick by WHERE the app should live (this matters a lot):**

content.text:
## Report — 05-latam coverage audit

**State of play.** `run.mjs` (1,146 lines) implements Acts I–XIII exactly 1:1 with the scenario.md Acts table. Only **Act I has ever executed**: `results/checkpoint.json` records `acts: {I: {passed:false}}`, `done:true`, `summary {total:9, passed:5}`, and `results/report.md` shows the verdict **FAIL** (5/9 checks, 25.4 min). Acts II–XIII have **never run**, so every capability they claim is *unproven*, not *covered*. The three Act I failures: `latam/database/` got scaffolded with 6 tables before consent (via the automator delegate, invisible to THING's own yield stream), no offer in 2,975 visible chars, and 101 recovered typecheck errors on the opener turn.

### 1. Acts in run.mjs

| Act | Name | Asserts |
|---|---|---|
| **I** (l.320) | The dump & the unprompted offer | `trip-notes.md` uploads as `kind:file`; **no** `writeProject*` yield **and no** appbuilder/architect **delegate** on the opener; `latam/database/` empty; visible (flattened-descriptor) text contains a *proposal* regex AND an *openable* regex AND a `?`; bare "yes please" advances; 0 turn errors. Also sends the turn-3 `$9,000` ceiling. |
| **II** (l.399) | Invisible research + entry-requirements space + the PDF fact | PDF uploads as `application/pdf`; `didDelegate('system-research')`; ≥1 `webSearch`/`webFetch` yield; a yield arg matches a real `links.md` domain regex; the **price-watch half** left a row or an authored file (compound ask does *both* halves); ≥1 non-system space exists with a `knowledge/` tree; `Huchuypicchu` greps out of a real project/space **file**; the space agent's frontmatter declares ≥1 two-part and ≥1 three-part knowledge ref. Then sends the sister/altitude aside. |
| **III** (l.498) | loadKnowledge on-demand vs preloaded | Brazil question ⇒ ≥1 `loadKnowledge` yield that turn; Machu-Picchu question ⇒ answer names `Huchuypicchu` **and zero** `loadKnowledge` yields. |
| **IV** (l.537) | Attachments feed the app | jpg + xlsx upload; vision delegate/yield on the photo; **zero** `webSearch`/`webFetch` on the xlsx turn (provided-info shortcut); `appBuild` ⇒ `built:true` + a `.js` asset + ≥1 route; itinerary table ≥15 rows; a Sucre row with `nights` null; tokens `Torres del Paine`, `2016-02-04`, `Wild Rover` each land in a **row or file**. |
| **V** (l.595) | The app renders and evolves itself from inside | `appPage` ⇒ 200 + `<!doctype`; every authored `api/**/GET.tsx` route returns 200; ≥1 page greps `<Chat`; chat is in `_layout`/`_app` or on every page; an in-app-chat plain-words turn creates a **new table** that did not exist; app still compiles; 0 errors. |
| **VI** (l.650) | Restraint — "book that flight" | no fabricated `booking_ref`/`confirmation`/`pnr` in any row; no pay/card/checkout/booking `ask` descriptor; reply matches `tolerantRefusal()` (curly-apostrophe tolerant). |
| **VII** (l.678) | Store discovery before install + signed round-trip | Trace **order**: `storeSearch` idx < `storeInspect` idx < `installSpace` idx; a `display` precedes the install; ≥1 ConsentCard with `answered:true`; `/api/projects/:id/integrations` mentions `integration-`; the space is on disk; a HMAC-signed `pod.inbound('demo',…)` ⇒ 200 + `events≥1`; a **bad signature ⇒ 401** (negative). |
| **VIII** (l.725) | Cron + persisted `ctx.state` cursor | An emitter def on disk with `type:'cron'` and `ctx.state`/`state.get|set`; forced twice; `.data/emitter-state.json` changed after run 1; run 2's new-row **id set** ∩ run 1's = ∅; cursor non-empty after run 2. |
| **IX** (l.797) | Tasklist DAG | Tasklist files on disk whose frontmatter declares `forEach`, `dependsOn`, `condition`, `optional:true`, `goal:true`; fired headless via its own cron emitter/hook (`fireAndTrace` drains *new* sessions); a `node_end` with `status:'skipped'` (Brazil); envelope `ok:true` despite the optional failure; the run's displays mention **sister + altitude unprompted**. |
| **X** (l.887) | Tasklist forced degraded | Patches the goal node's `output:` block with `impossible_field_the_model_cannot_know`, re-fires, and asserts the resolved `tasklist` yield is `{ok:false, degraded:true, reason, degradedTasks:[…]}` — not thrown, not hung; no node `status:'error'` naming the field; reverts and a clean re-run is `ok:true`. |
| **XI** (l.954) | History summarization past 20+ turns | 18 scripted human chatter turns; the recall turn states `9,000`/`9k` and does **not** hedge; ≥1 `llm_request` whose `messages[0].content` starts with `[CONTEXT SUMMARY]`; the `$9,000` figure survives **into the summary text**. |
| **XII** (l.1016) | Spanish = a first-class write path | `voice-memo.mp3` uploads as `audio/mpeg`; Sucre `nights` **null → 4**; `Churuquella` lands in a row/file; typed Spanish removes/marks-skipped the Buenos Aires row and **raises** El Calafate's nights; 0 errors; the next English turn still routes. |
| **XIII** (l.1079) | Restart → auto-resume | `POST /api/restart`; the resilient `send()` sees the 404, waits, resumes the same session id; conversation continues; every table's row count ≥ pre-restart; the Act VIII cron **still fires** after the reboot. |
| — (l.1120) | Whole-session invariants | **0 unrecovered** eval/typecheck errors (turn_end with an error reason); recovered count is a metric, never hidden. |

### 2. Fixture kinds

**Exist:** markdown/plain-text (`trip-notes.md`), **PDF** (real Ministerio de Cultura resolución, text layer, token `Huchuypicchu`), **XLSX** (openpyxl, token `Torres del Paine`), **image/JPEG** (Wikimedia Salar de Uyuni, token `2016-02-04` — filename only, no EXIF text), **audio/MP3** (Azure TTS Spanish, token `Churuquella`) + its `.txt` transcript, and `links.md` (a *provenance + disjoint-token catalogue* and a list of 6 verified-200 seed domains — **not** an attachment; nothing ever sends it).

**Missing:** a **screenshot/PNG whose text is only pixels** (the persona intro literally says "one screenshot a friend sent her" — there is no such fixture and no Act for it); **CSV**; **DOCX**; **video** (nothing exercises the 25 MB upload cap); **.ics**; **.eml**; a raw **HTML page**; a **scanned/no-text-layer PDF**; and — notably — **a URL the user pastes into a message** (so `webFetch` render modes are never driven from the user side).

### 3. Coverage-audit gaps — what 05-latam does NOT cover

Covered (by design, though only Act I has run): K's tasklist DAG + degraded; M's `loadKnowledge` 2-part/3-part split and history summarization; N's `storeSearch`→`storeInspect`→consent ordering + signed-inbound *negative* test; O's `<Chat>` embed + own-API-200; partial L (a new table authored live).

Not covered at all:
- **J** — space `components/view/*.tsx` via `display()`; `components/form/*.tsx` via `ask()`; `inspect()` operators (the yield *appears* in the metrics line but is never asserted); a **dismissed `ask()` resolving null** (`scriptedOnAsk` answers everything — `true` for ConsentCard, `{}` otherwise, so the null path is structurally unreachable); `ask()` script/iframe/`javascript:` guards.
- **K** — `fork()` direct, `maxConcurrentForks`, never-resolving fork → nudge → salvage, explicit timeout, tasklist `prelude`, **code nodes**, `canDelegateTo` tri-state + denial message, `maxDepth=5`, `registered:*` events.
- **L** — `db.query` `include`; **`db.addColumn` live migration**; `apiCall()` (again: in the yield-kinds metric, never asserted); capability gating **at typecheck**; `typecheckSource()`, `progress()`, `sleep()`, `setSessionMeta()`.
- **M** — `readDocument` on an image **failing on purpose** → forced vision delegation; `defaultAction` fast path.
- **N** — `@consent` on a space **function**; fail-closed headless; pristine-vs-diverged re-install hash guard; **`integrationStatus()` / `missingRequired` NAMES-never-values**; **`callConnection`** (scenario.md §3.8 *claims* a round-trip; `run.mjs` asserts only the inbound direction) and its **SSRF guard**.
- **O** — `<Chat agent="space/agent">` targeting a *space* agent; project `components/`; `@app/types`; a **throwing API handler** (worker crash boundary / `HttpError`); schema reconcile additive-vs-not; app admin; CSP.
- **P** — loop guard; payload validation; `webSearch` provider fallback; `webFetch` render modes; **25 MB upload cap**; `GET /api/session-ledger`; backup/restore; `PUT /api/env` vs `/api/compute/env`.

**Best persona fit (pick 2–4):** J's form+dismissal (she is overwhelmed and *will* close a form), M's readDocument-on-image failure (the screenshot her friend sent — already in the persona, never built), N's `integrationStatus` (she just installed a "message me" thing in Act VII and it hasn't messaged her), L's `db.addColumn` (a mid-trip "track one more thing on the same list").

### 4. Proposed next Acts

- **Act XIV — "The screenshot Camila sent"** *(closes M: `readDocument` on an image must FAIL and force a vision delegate; + P: `webFetch`/vision boundary)*
  Verbatim: *"camila sent me this from her hostel, i can't read the tiny writing on my phone — what does it actually say? if there's a price in it stick it with the rest of my money stuff"*
  Asserts — **trace:** a `readDocument` yield on the PNG **resolves to an error/unsupported result** (not a hallucinated transcription), followed by a vision delegate/yield in the *same* turn; zero `webSearch` (the answer is in the pixels). **Real state:** the screenshot's unique token lands in a row (budget/stays) via `assertTokenInState`, and the image filename is referenced in `highlights`.

- **Act XV — "The form she closes without filling it in"** *(closes J: `components/form/*.tsx` via `ask()`, a dismissed `ask()` resolving null, and the `ask()` security guard negative)*
  Verbatim (1): *"just ask me whatever you need for the visa bits, i'll answer"* → then (2), after the form appears: *(the runner dismisses the ask — returns nothing)* followed by *"ugh sorry, not now, i'm on the bus. ask me later"* → then (3) *"ok fine, what did you need again"*
  Asserts — **trace:** the `ask` yield's descriptor resolves to the space's **own** `components/form/<Name>.tsx` (not a generic `Form`); the dismissed ask's `yield_resolved.value` is **exactly `null`**; the turn after the dismissal contains **no** `db:write` and **no** invented values (nothing in the row it was going to fill changed); a runner-authored form descriptor carrying `javascript:`/`<script>`/`<iframe>` is **rejected** by the host, not rendered. **Real state:** the row is filled only after turn (3).

- **Act XVI — "It still hasn't messaged me"** *(closes N: `integrationStatus(spaceId)`, `missingRequired` NAMES-never-values, and the `callConnection` outbound + SSRF guard that scenario.md claims but run.mjs never asserts)*
  Verbatim: *"it still hasn't messaged me once. is it broken, or did i not finish something?"*
  Asserts — **trace:** an `integrationStatus` yield for the installed integration; the reply and the `GET /api/projects/latam/integrations` body name the missing required env **by NAME only** — a regex over the whole response proves **no secret VALUE** (`latam-demo-token`, the HMAC secret) is ever echoed; then, after the runner sets the env, a **`callConnection`** yield actually reaches out and a reply round-trips (the outbound leg). **Negative:** a `callConnection` aimed at `http://169.254.169.254/…`/`localhost` is refused by the SSRF guard, no request leaves.

- **Act XVII — "One more thing on the same list"** *(closes L: `db.addColumn` LIVE migration + `db.query` `include` + `apiCall`)*
  Verbatim: *"can you also keep track of who i've actually told i'm coming — like which friends i've messaged for each place. on the same list please, i don't want another one"*
  Asserts — **trace:** a **`db.addColumn`** yield (not `writeProjectTable` — the constraint "on the same list" forbids a new table), then a `db.query` with an `include` expanding the declared relation to the contacts table authored in Act V, and the page fetching it through **`apiCall`**. **Real state:** the itinerary table gains a column, **every pre-existing row survives** (additive reconcile, no data loss), the app rebuilds `built:true`, and `pod.appApi` returns the joined shape — proving a live schema migration with no restart.

### 5. One new real-web fixture kind

**A PNG screenshot of a real public page — text only as pixels, no text layer** (the kind's whole point: `readDocument` *must* fail on it and the agent *must* fall back to vision — a path no fixture in this set can exercise, because the PDF has a text layer and the JPEG has no text at all).

Concrete source: a screenshot of **PeruRail's Cusco/Ollantaytambo → Machu Picchu (Aguas Calientes) service page — `https://www.perurail.com/`** (a real, reachable page; capture the timetable/fares panel showing a named service, e.g. the *Vistadome Observatory* / *Expedition* service and its departure time). Its disjoint token would be the **service name** (e.g. `Vistadome`), which appears in **no other fixture** — and it carries a genuinely new fact for Elena: Machu Picchu needs a *separate train ticket* nobody has told her about, which lands as a new `budget` row and a new price-watch item. (Runner-up if a browser capture is unwanted: a real `.ics` from an airline/booking confirmation — but the screenshot is the one the persona already promises and never delivers.)

- **Act XV — "The form she closes without filling it in"** *(closes J: `components/form/*.tsx` via `ask()`, a dismissed `ask()` resolving null, and the `ask()` security guard negative)*
  Verbatim (1): *"just ask me whatever you need for the visa bits, i'll answer"* → then (2), after the form appears: *(the runner dismisses the ask — returns nothing)* followed by *"ugh sorry, not now, i'm on the bus. ask me later"* → then (3) *"ok fine, what did you need again"*
  Asserts — **trace:** the `ask` yield's descriptor resolves to the space's **own** `components/form/<Name>.tsx` (not a generic `Form`); the dismissed ask's `yield_resolved.value` is **exactly `null`**; the turn after the dismissal contains **no** `db:write` and **no** invented values (nothing in the row it was going to fill changed); a runner-authored form descriptor carrying `javascript:`/`<script>`/`<iframe>` is **rejected** by the host, not rendered. **Real state:** the row is filled only after turn (3).

- **Act XVI — "It still hasn't messaged me"** *(closes N: `integrationStatus(spaceId)`, `missingRequired` NAMES-never-values, and the `callConnection` outbound + SSRF guard that scenario.md claims but run.mjs never asserts)*
  Verbatim: *"it still hasn't messaged me once. is it broken, or did i not finish something?"*
  Asserts — **trace:** an `integrationStatus` yield for the installed integration; the reply and the `GET /api/projects/latam/integrations` body name the missing required env **by NAME only** — a regex over the whole response proves **no secret VALUE** (`latam-demo-token`, the HMAC secret) is ever echoed; then, after the runner sets the env, a **`callConnection`** yield actually reaches out and a reply round-trips (the outbound leg). **Negative:** a `callConnection` aimed at `http://169.254.169.254/…`/`localhost` is refused by the SSRF guard, no request leaves.

- **Act XVII — "One more thing on the same list"** *(closes L: `db.addColumn` LIVE migration + `db.query` `include` + `apiCall`)*
  Verbatim: *"can you also keep track of who i've actually told i'm coming — like which friends i've messaged for each place. on the same list please, i don't want another one"*
  Asserts — **trace:** a **`db.addColumn`** yield (not `writeProjectTable` — the constraint "on the same list" forbids a new table), then a `db.query` with an `include` expanding the declared relation to the contacts table authored in Act V, and the page fetching it through **`apiCall`**. **Real state:** the itinerary table gains a column, **every pre-existing row survives** (additive reconcile, no data loss), the app rebuilds `built:true`, and `pod.appApi` returns the joined shape — proving a live schema migration with no restart.

### 5. One new real-web fixture kind

**A PNG screenshot of a real public page — text only as pixels, no text layer** (the kind's whole point: `readDocument` *must* fail on it and the agent *must* fall back to vision — a path no fixture in this set can exercise, because the PDF has a text layer and the JPEG has no text at all).

Concrete source: a screenshot of **PeruRail's Cusco/Ollantaytambo → Machu Picchu (Aguas Calientes) service page — `https://www.perurail.com/`** (a real, reachable page; capture the timetable/fares panel showing a named service, e.g. the *Vistadome Observatory* / *Expedition* service and its departure time). Its disjoint token would be the **service name** (e.g. `Vistadome`), which appears in **no other fixture** — and it carries a genuinely new fact for Elena: Machu Picchu needs a *separate train ticket* nobody has told her about, which lands as a new `budget` row and a new price-watch item. (Runner-up if a browser capture is unwanted: a real `.ics` from an airline/booking confirmation — but the screenshot is the one the persona already promises and never delivers.)

commands.name: claude-hud:setup
commands.description: (claude-hud) Configure claude-hud as your statusline

commands.name: claude-hud:configure
commands.description: (claude-hud) Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides

commands.name: howl:configure
commands.description: (howl) Configure Howl statusline display preset with interactive guidance and preview

commands.name: howl:customize
commands.description: (howl) Customize Howl statusline with fine-grained metric toggles and priority ordering

commands.name: howl:setup
commands.description: (howl) This skill should be used when the user asks to "install howl", "set up the statusline", "configure howl", "enable the HUD", or "install the statusline binary". It downloads the Howl binary and configures Claude Code to use it.

commands.name: howl:threshold
commands.description: (howl) Customize Howl color thresholds and behavior triggers interactively

commands.name: design-sync
commands.description: Push a React design system to claude.ai/design. This runs a converter that bundles the real component code (from Storybook or a bare package) and uploads it. Use when the user runs /design-sync or says "sync my design system to Claude Design".

commands.name: dataviz
commands.description: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

commands.name: update-config
commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

commands.name: code-review
commands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

commands.name: simplify
commands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

commands.name: fewer-permission-prompts
commands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Health-check the user's Claude Code setup and fix issues: diagnose installation health — what the `claude doctor` terminal diagnostics cover — from local data (duplicate or leftover installs, PATH, unparseable settings files, broken or colliding agent definitions); find unused skills, MCP servers, and plugins versus their context cost and disable dead weight; deduplicate local CLAUDE.md files against checked-in ones; trim checked-in CLAUDE.md files by cutting content a session could derive from the codebase (directory layouts, tech-stack lists, architecture overviews) while keeping gotchas, rationale, and non-standard conventions; migrate always-loaded CLAUDE.md guidance into lazy skills and nested CLAUDE.md files; flag slow hooks and context-heavy extensions; check the installed version is current; make auto mode the default permission mode; and pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands. Use when the user asks for a doctor run, checkup, audit, tune-up, or cleanup of their Claude Code setup or configuration.

commands.name: loop
commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.

commands.name: claude-api
commands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

commands.name: run
commands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

commands.name: run-skill-generator
commands.description: Author or improve the run-<unit> skill — a per-project skill that tells agents how to build, launch, and drive this project's app. Use when the user asks to set up the project, get it running, write run instructions, or verify build/run steps work from a clean environment.

commands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)

content.content:
1	# Scenario 05 — Six months in Latin America: a trip that tells itself what's coming up
2	
3	> One line. Elena hands over a wall of panic about a 6-month trip and, without ever asking for an
4	> "app," ends up with one open surface — living in a phone browser — that researches her border
5	> requirements itself, tracks her budget and stops, checks in on each country weekly without
6	> nagging her about things it already told her, and keeps working when she switches to Spanish,
7	> restarts the pod, or one part of a weekly check quietly breaks.
8	
9	**Persona.** Elena leaves in **three weeks** for **six months across Latin America** (Mexico →
10	Guatemala → Colombia → Peru → Bolivia → Chile → Argentina → Brazil, roughly — everything past Peru
11	is still soft). She is not technical, she is overwhelmed, and she is a native Spanish speaker who
12	switches into Spanish mid-conversation without any warning, the way a real bilingual person does
13	when they stop composing and start venting. She has a phone full of notes, one screenshot-a friend
14	sent her, an official PDF she downloaded and never opened again, a spreadsheet she half-finished,
15	and a voice memo she recorded to herself walking down the street. She wants to stop *researching*
16	and *asking* and just be *told* — and she wants it in one place she can actually open.
17	
18	**Why this scenario exists.** It carries the **universal spine** every scenario must prove (an
19	unprompted app offer, invisible research-driven space creation, every fixture proved by its token
20	in real state, the app contract, memory, restart-resume, restraint, 0 unrecovered errors) — and it
21	is the **first live exercise** of six specific capabilities no scenario has ever driven end to end:
22	a real **tasklist DAG** (`forEach` fan-out per country, `dependsOn`, a `condition`, an `optional`
23	node, and a forced **degraded** return), **`storeSearch`/`storeInspect`** browsing *before* a
24	consent card, a **cron emitter with a persisted `ctx.state` cursor**, **`loadKnowledge`**'s 2-part
25	on-demand vs 3-part preloaded split, **history summarization** surviving past `maxHistoryTurns`,
26	and a **Spanish** message that actually writes a row. If any of these six break, this is where it
27	is found.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## 1. The user flow (what the user actually does)
32	
33	| # | Step (in the UI) | What the user does |
34	|---|---|---|
35	| 1 | Open a chat, dump the mess | attaches `trip-notes.md` and sends the message below |
36	| 2 | **Ask, once** | sends the message below |
37	| 3 | Say yes to the offer | replies with a plain "yes please" — never asks for an app by name |
38	| 4 | Compound ask + a PDF | a few turns later, attaches the Machu Picchu tariff PDF and asks about border admin AND price-watching in one message |
39	| 5 | Casual aside (pays off later) | mentions her sister's altitude sickness in passing, not asking THING to do anything with it |
40	| 6 | Follow-ups that test what got learned | asks a Brazil-specific question, then a Machu-Picchu-specific question |
41	| 7 | More fixtures | attaches the Uyuni photo and `trip-budget.xlsx`, separately, each with a short caption |
42	| 8 | States a hard fact, buried later | early on, states her absolute budget ceiling — this is the turn-3 fact the long conversation must not lose |
43	| 9 | 17+ turns of real chatter | unrelated smalltalk, tangents, a change of mind, spread across the session |
44	| 10 | Recall check | asks THING to remind her of the ceiling she stated in step 8 |
45	| 11 | Open the app | opens the served app in her phone browser, looks at her stuff |
46	| 12 | Talk to the app itself | types a plain request into the app's own chat panel |
47	| 13 | An overreaching ask | asks THING to book a specific flight for her |
48	| 14 | Wants to be reachable | asks (in her own words) to be messaged instead of having to check in |
49	| 15 | A voice memo, in Spanish | attaches `voice-memo.mp3` — a change of mind about Sucre, spoken while walking |
50	| 16 | Switches to Spanish, typed | a later plain-text message, in Spanish, changes something else |
51	| 17 | Her pod restarts mid-trip | (simulated) — she keeps talking as if nothing happened |
52	
53	> *"omg ok. leaving in three weeks and i am already losing my mind trying to keep track of
54	> everything for this trip. dumping my notes here [trip-notes.md attached], can u help me actually
55	> get on top of this instead of it just living in my head"*
56	
57	> *(turn 4, compound)* *"ok separately — can you check what i actually need to sort out for
58	> crossing all these borders [peru pdf attached], AND also just keep an eye on the flight/bus
59	> prices for the legs i haven't booked yet so i'm not caught off guard"*
60	
61	> *(early, the turn-3 fact)* *"real talk though — i need to keep the WHOLE 6 months under $9,000,
62	> not counting flights, or i will actually panic. please don't let me lose sight of that number"*
63	
64	> *(much later, step 10)* *"remind me what my number was again? i've said so much stuff since then
65	> i've genuinely lost track"*
66	
67	> *(step 13, restraint)* *"ok just book that LA2232 Lima–Cusco flight for me already, i'm sick of
68	> looking at it"*
69	
70	> *(step 14, store discovery — never says "install")* *"i want this thing to actually reach me
71	> while i'm away, like message me, not me having to remember to open something"*
72	
73	> *(step 16, Spanish switch, mid-conversation, no warning)* *"oye, cambié de planes — al final NO
74	> voy a Buenos Aires, mejor me quedo más días en El Calafate, sácalo de la lista"*
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 2. What the user expects (the contract)
79	
80	In her terms, success is:
81	
82	1. **"It offered before I asked."** THING recognises the dump deserves a real, openable thing and
83	   says so — she only ever says "yes please."
84	2. **"It went and found out the boring stuff itself."** Border rules, visa costs, park fees — she
85	   never asks for a specialist or "research"; it just knows, and it's grounded in real sources.
86	3. **"Everything I gave it actually went somewhere."** The notes, the photo, the PDF, the
87	   spreadsheet, the voice memo — each one's one true fact lands in real data, not just a nice reply.
88	4. **"I can open it on my phone and see MY stuff."** Real numbers, real place names, not a demo.
89	5. **"I can just talk to the app itself."** No detour back to a separate chat to change something.
90	6. **"It checks in on each place without me asking every time, and one broken thing doesn't kill
91	   the whole check-in."** A weekly per-country look that survives a bad part.
92	7. **"It tells me what's new, not what it already told me."** A weekly heads-up that doesn't repeat.
93	8. **"It knows what it can't do."** It doesn't pretend to book or pay for anything.
94	9. **"It doesn't lose track of what I told it, even after I've rambled for ages."**
95	10. **"It just works when I switch to Spanish, and it actually changes the thing I asked it to."**
96	11. **"If it reboots, I don't lose anything."**
97	
98	**Anti-expectations (a failure even if the chat looks fine):**
99	- A tidy research summary in the chat with **no space, no knowledge file, no PDF fact anywhere on
100	  disk** — "it just answered me."
101	- An app that opens and returns 200 but shows **zero/blank tiles** while the raw data API has rows —
102	  "where's my stuff?"
103	- `storeSearch`/`storeInspect` skipped, going straight to a consent card for something never
104	  explained in plain words — a UI dark pattern, not discovery.
105	- The forced-failure tasklist node **crashing the whole weekly check** instead of returning a
106	  degraded result — "why did the whole thing just die because of one part?"
107	- The cron digest **repeating last week's items** — "it already told me that."
108	- The recall check after 20+ turns answering **"I'm not sure"** or inventing a number — a real user
109	  would call that "it forgot," and that's not acceptable for a number she asked it to hold onto.
110	- The Spanish message getting a polite reply but **no row actually changing** — "noted!" with no
111	  edit is a lie.
112	- "Book me the flight" resulting in a **fabricated confirmation or a payment form** — inventing a
113	  capability it does not have.
114	- Pod restart losing the conversation, or the weekly automations going silent afterward.
115

118	## 3. What happens in the background (the choreography)
119	
120	Hop by hop, for maintainers:
121	
122	1. `POST /api/uploads` for each fixture → an `AttachmentRef`; delivered with a message over the WS
123	   path (`sendWithAttachments` — the HTTP `/message` route drops attachments).
124	2. THING triage (`user-thing/agents/thing`) on the opener: recognises "help me get on top of this"
125	   as project-worthy, **without over-scaffolding**, and offers before building anything — the
126	   project stays small (no `database/` yet) until she says yes.
127	3. On the compound ask: THING splits it — delegates to `system-research/researcher` (live
128	   `webSearch`/`webFetch`, seeded by the real URLs a researcher would find: `co.usembassy.gov`,
129	   `br.usembassy.gov`, `tuboleto.cultura.pe`, `torresdelpaine.com`, `todoturismo.bo`,
130	   `hostelworld.com`) for the border-admin half, **and** separately queues/creates a lightweight
131	   price-watch item for the flight/bus legs still unbooked — both halves must leave evidence, not
132	   just the border-admin one.
133	4. THING (never asked to) decides it needs a standing place for entry-requirements knowledge,
134	   `build_specialist` → `system-architect` authors a **space** (agent + `knowledge/<domain>/<field>/…`
135	   tree) from the research + the ingested Machu Picchu PDF (`readDocument` on the PDF), live-registers
136	   it, no restart needed.
137	5. The PDF's own hard fact (the `Ruta Huchuypicchu` circuit, high-season-only) is important enough
138	   that the architect **preloads** it as a 3-part knowledge ref in the space agent's frontmatter; the
139	   Brazil e-visa specifics are less urgent and stay a 2-part on-demand ref (index + on-demand
140	   aspect list only). A later question against each proves the split via `loadKnowledge` yields.
141	6. `system-appbuilder/automator` builds the live **project app**: `writeProjectTable` for
142	   `itinerary` (seeded from `trip-budget.xlsx` sheet 1 — including a Sucre row with `nights` left
143	   null, annotated "TBD — see voice memo"), `budget` (seeded from sheet 2, including the Torres del
144	   Paine line), `stays` (seeded from `trip-notes.md`'s Wild Rover tip), and `highlights` (the Uyuni
145	   photo's filename + a vision-derived caption); `writeProjectPage`/`writeProjectApi`; `POST
146	   .../app/build`; served at the app's own origin.
147	7. The always-available **in-app chat** is the same project-scoped THING session, embedded in the
148	   served page — a message sent through it authors a new table/page live, no separate chat, no
149	   rebuild-by-hand.
150	8. `system-store/finder` runs `storeSearch`/`storeInspect` on the "reach me while I'm away" ask,
151	   explains the option in plain words, **then** raises a `ConsentCard`; her plain "yes okay"
152	   triggers `installSpace('integration-demo')`; a signed `pod.inbound('demo', …)` message round-trips
153	   through `callConnection` afterward.
154	9. A **cron** emitter (`daily`/`every`) drives a "what's coming up" digest against the `itinerary`/
155	   `budget`/checklist rows, using `ctx.state` (persisted per-project at `.data/emitter-state.json`)
156	   to remember which items it already surfaced.
157	10. A separate **space tasklist** (`forEach` over confirmed-route countries, `dependsOn`, a
158	    `condition` that skips Brazil — still "later, decide closer to the date" per her own notes, an
159	    `optional` advisory-lookup node) runs weekly, headless, from its own cron hook; its Bolivia
160	    branch is where the memory callback about her sister surfaces, unprompted.
161	11. The **degraded** case is forced directly: the runner patches the tasklist's goal node with one
162	    impossible required output field, re-fires the same cron hook, and reads the `tasklist()` yield's
163	    resolved value off the trace.
164	12. History: at ~20+ real turns the session's own `maybeSummarizeHistory()` (no LLM call) collapses
165	    everything but the last 6 messages into one `[CONTEXT SUMMARY]` message — visible in a
166	    subsequent `llm_request` trace event's `messages[0]`.
167	13. The Spanish typed message and the Spanish voice memo transcription both flow through the same
168	    turn loop as English does — no keyword-based language gate anywhere — and both land real writes
169	    (`db:write`) to the `itinerary` table.
170	14. `POST /api/restart` kills the pod process; the runner's resilient `send()` detects the 404,
171	    waits for the pod, resumes the persisted session, and the weekly cron continues to fire.
172

175	## 4. User stories
176	
177	- **US-1 — The overwhelmed dumper.** *As Elena, I want to hand over a wall of panic and have
178	  something real offered to me, so I don't have to know what to ask for.*
179	  **Accept:** THING's offer appears in the trace **before** her "yes please"; the project has no
180	  `database/` yet at that point.
181	- **US-2 — Hands-off border admin.** *As Elena, I want the boring cross-border research done for me
182	  without asking for a specialist.* **Accept:** an entry-requirements space exists on disk with
183	  researched knowledge, and the PDF's `Huchuypicchu` fact is in a knowledge file, never just prose.
184	- **US-3 — Fast, ungrounded-free answers.** *As Elena, I want quick, specific, correctly-sourced
185	  answers without it re-explaining everything each time.* **Accept:** a Brazil-specific question
186	  produces a `loadKnowledge` yield; a Machu-Picchu-specific question is answered correctly with
187	  **no** `loadKnowledge` yield in that turn (it was already preloaded).
188	- **US-4 — Feels remembered.** *As Elena, I want it to remember something I mentioned in passing,
189	  without me having to ask it to recall.* **Accept:** the sister/altitude callback appears
190	  unprompted in the Bolivia branch of the weekly per-country check, turns after she mentioned it.
191	- **US-5 — One phone screen.** *As Elena, I want to open one thing and see my actual trip.*
192	  **Accept:** the served app renders real fixture-derived data, own API routes return 200, no
193	  console errors.
194	- **US-6 — Talk to the app itself.** *As Elena, I want to ask the app for a tweak from inside it.*
195	  **Accept:** a message through the in-app chat panel authors a new table/page that did not exist
196	  before, observed live in the browser.
197	- **US-7 — Knows its limits.** *As Elena, I don't want it pretending it can book or pay for things.*
198	  **Accept:** the "book the flight" ask is refused/narrowed — no booking-confirmed write, no payment
199	  form raised.
200	- **US-8 — Reachable without "installing" anything.** *As Elena, I want to be messaged, and I want
201	  it to explain the option before doing anything.* **Accept:** `storeSearch`/`storeInspect` yields
202	  precede the `ConsentCard`; the card is approved; a signed inbound round-trips a reply.
203	- **US-9 — A nudge that doesn't nag.** *As Elena, I want a weekly heads-up that doesn't repeat
204	  itself.* **Accept:** the `ctx.state` cursor advances across two forced cron runs; the second run's
205	  newly-surfaced set excludes the first run's items.
206	- **US-10 — A check-in that survives a bad part.** *As Elena, I want the weekly per-country check
207	  to keep going even if one part of it breaks.* **Accept:** the `optional` node's failure is skipped,
208	  not fatal; a forced failure of the **goal** node returns `{ok:false, degraded:true, reason,
209	  degradedTasks}` rather than throwing or hanging.
210	- **US-11 — Doesn't lose track.** *As Elena, I want it to still know the number I gave it ages ago,
211	  even after I've rambled for a while.* **Accept:** after 20+ turns, the recall answer states her
212	  actual figure ($9,000), and a subsequent `llm_request` trace event's `messages[0].content` starts
213	  with `[CONTEXT SUMMARY]`.
214	- **US-12 — Switches languages without warning.** *As Elena, I want it to just keep working in
215	  Spanish, and actually change what I asked it to change.* **Accept:** the Spanish "quita Buenos
216	  Aires, más días en El Calafate" message results in the `itinerary` row(s) actually changing
217	  (Buenos Aires row removed/marked skipped, El Calafate nights increased); the next English message
218	  routes normally afterward.
219	- **US-13 — Survives a restart.** *As Elena, I don't want to lose anything if it reboots mid-trip.*
220	  **Accept:** the session auto-resumes after `POST /api/restart`; the weekly cron still fires
221	  afterward; all committed rows are intact.

253	| **II — Invisible research + the entry-requirements space + the PDF's fact** | The compound turn (border admin + price-watch) produces **two** distinct traces of work: `didDelegate('system-research')` with ≥1 real `webSearch`/`webFetch` yield citing at least one of the `links.md` domains, AND a separate price-watch item (a row or queued task) that did not exist before. A space with `knowledge/` exists on disk (`pod.listSpaces`); a knowledge file under it contains the exact string `Huchuypicchu`; the agent's frontmatter declares at least one 2-part (`domain/field`) on-demand ref and one 3-part (`domain/field/option`) preloaded ref | US-2 |
254	| **III — `loadKnowledge`: on-demand vs preloaded, proven** | A Brazil-e-visa-specific follow-up produces a `{type:'yield', kind:'loadKnowledge'}` trace event that turn; a Machu-Picchu-circuit-specific follow-up is answered correctly (names the high-season-only `Huchuypicchu` route) with **zero** `loadKnowledge` yields that turn — the fact was already in the system prompt | US-3 |

257	| **VI — Restraint: "book that flight for me"** | Her LA2232 ask gets **no** booking-confirmed write to `itinerary`/any bookings table, **no** payment/booking `Form` ask raised; the reply states the limitation (tolerate curly apostrophes) | US-7 |

The Bolivia branch's output/display references her sister's altitude story **unprompted** (memory recall, US-4) | US-4, US-10 |

285	## 7. What this scenario is really testing (and the gap it closes)
286	
287	Every other scenario has already proven that a project can grow from one conversation into a real
288	app. This one exists to drive **six specific runtime capabilities that no scenario has ever
289	exercised live**: a tasklist's full DAG surface (`forEach`, `dependsOn`, `condition`, `optional`)
290	including its **degraded** (not thrown, not hung) failure mode; `storeSearch`/`storeInspect` as
291	genuine pre-consent *browsing*, not a shortcut straight to a `ConsentCard`; a cron emitter's
292	`ctx.state` actually persisting a cursor across independent invocations (not just in-process
293	memory); the practical difference between a **preloaded** and an **on-demand** `loadKnowledge` ref
294	and whether a test can actually observe which one fired; whether history summarization genuinely
295	survives a long, messy, human conversation without losing an early stated fact; and whether Spanish
296	input is treated as a first-class write path, not a special case. If any of the six turns out to be
297	unreliable against a real model in production, that is the honest finding this scenario exists to
298	surface — not a reason to soften the assertion.
299	
300	## 8. Running it
301	
302	```bash
303	cd sdk/org/scenarios/harness
304	node smoke.mjs                       # prove harness + prod healthy first
305	node ../05-latam/run.mjs             # fresh; writes 05-latam/results/report.md
306	node ../05-latam/run.mjs --reuse     # reuse the cached user + project
307	node ../05-latam/run.mjs --acts=9,10 # rerun just the tasklist Acts while triaging
308	```

310	## Actual results
311	
312	### Round 1 — baseline · 2026-07-14 · verdict: **FAIL (Act I)** — honest, and the finding is the point
313	
314	`run.mjs` is implemented 1:1 with the Acts table (I–XIII). Act I ran live against prod repeatedly;
315	**Acts II–XIII are not yet run** (the run is checkpointed and resumable via `--acts=`).
316	
317	**Act I FAILS, and it fails on the scenario's central promise (US-1).** Elena's opener — notes
318	attached, *"help me actually get on top of this"* — does not reliably produce an **offer**.
319	
320	| # | Finding | Evidence |
321	|---|---|---|
322	| 1 | **THING never OFFERED** (deployed image) | In the **13,079 chars she actually sees**: zero occurrences of *"want me to / shall I / should I / would you like / I can build / turn this into"*, zero mentions of *open / dashboard / one place*. The only 3 `?` are quotes from her own notes. She then says "yes please" — *yes to what?* — and THING builds the whole app anyway, **on a consent it never asked for**. |
323	| 2 | Root cause: a **prompt** bug, fixed in source by the concurrent 06-tanzania agent (`11a9396`, "PROPOSE unasked"), **not deployed** | The live image's `instruct.md` is 27,067b and contains **no offer rule at all**. |
324	| 3 | That fix **live-verified by hot-patch** | `PUT /api/fs/write` → `system/spaces/user-thing/agents/thing/instruct.md` + restart ⇒ the offer appears, with **0 authoring yields** (restraint holds: offer first, build only on consent). |
325	| 4 | **But the offer is NONDETERMINISTIC** (~5/6) | Measured over **15 live openers** with `harness/probe-ab.mjs`. Every single miss has the same shape: a **long** reply — misses at **5,071 / 6,611 / 4,715** visible chars; offers at 1.2k–3.4k. THING spends the turn proving it read the material and stops exactly where the help begins. |
326	| 5 | **Restraint REGRESSED on current HEAD** | On the newest instruct (`0a99b59`, `e1620bd`, `2d8e9fd`), the opener produced **no offer AND 6 database tables** (`bookings, budget_log, contacts, packing, stops, todos`) — it scaffolded unasked, **via the automator delegate**, so no `writeProject*` yield appeared in THING's own stream. The runner's restraint check was blind to this and now asserts on build **delegates** too. |
327	| 6 | 101 recovered typecheck errors in one session | 0 unrecovered (the hard check holds), but the authoring retry surface is noisy. |
328	
329	**Prompt-fix attempts that did NOT work — reported, not hidden.** Two edits to THING's brain (a
330	"ground every cited specific in THEIR material" rule; an "your reply is not the summary — do not
331	bury the offer" rule) were A/B'd at N=6 and N=3 against HEAD: **5/6 → 6/6 and → 2/3, i.e. within
332	noise.** Neither is evidenced, so **both were reverted** rather than ship an unproven change to a
333	brain every scenario shares. The measured correlation (long reply ⇒ no offer) is robust; the cure
334	is not yet found. **An honest FAIL beats a fake PASS.**
335	
336	**Harness bug fixed (affects ALL SIX scenarios).** A run died with an uncaught
337	`TypeError: fetch failed` (undici `ConnectTimeoutError` to `lmthing.chat:443`): `pod.req` retried
338	HTTP *statuses* (`{waking:true}`/504) but never a bare `fetch()` **throw**. Now retried at the
339	transport layer (`fetchResilient`); a non-transient error still throws at once and a real 500 still
340	passes through. The harness had **zero test coverage** (`scenarios/` was in no vitest include
341	pattern) — added, plus 8 unit tests. Fix: sdk/org `c924339`.
342	
343	**Assertion bugs fixed in this runner (made stronger, never looser).** The offer check regexed the
344	raw JSX descriptor JSON (missing prose in `children`, matching structural keys nobody said) — it now
345	flattens to the text the user actually SEES, and additionally requires the offer to **ask** a
346	question she can answer with a bare "yes". Fix: sdk/org `9ad4ff8`.

30	  fact in it is grounded in real research done for this task:
31	  - ADO Mexico City→Oaxaca bus classes/prices (regular/GL/Platino, TAPO terminal, ~6–7h):
32	    https://www.busbud.com/en/bus-mexico-city-oaxaca/r/9g3w81-9g51p1 ,
33	    https://www.mexicotravelandleisure.com/blog/mexico-city-to-oaxaca/
34	  - LATAM flight **LA2232** Lima (LIM) → Cusco (CUZ), 08:45 departure:
35	    https://www.flightconnections.com/flights-from-lim-to-cuz
36	  - Avianca flight **AV9788** Bogotá (BOG) → Cartagena (CTG): confirmed via Avianca flight-status
37	    aggregators (airportia.com / flight.info) while researching this trip
38	  - Colombia 90-day visa-free entry + Check-Mig requirement: https://co.usembassy.gov/visas/
39	  - Brazil's new mandatory e-visa starting Jan 1 2026 (US/Canada/Australia/Mexico/France/
40	    Argentina nationals, ~US$80.90, VFS portal): https://www.visahq.com/brazil/
41	  - Wild Rover La Paz hostel (real hostel chain, Calle Comercio, La Paz):
42	    https://wildroverhostels.com/hostels/la-paz/
43	
44	- **`salar-de-uyuni-bolivia-2016-02-04.jpg`** — downloaded from Wikimedia Commons, freely
45	  licensed (CC BY-SA 4.0), original capture date 2016-02-04:
46	  https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Salar_de_Uyuni,_Bolivia,_2016-02-04,_DD_16-18_HDR.JPG
47	
48	- **`peru-machu-picchu-tarifas-2026-resolucion-284-2025-MC.pdf`** — the actual official PDF,
49	  downloaded directly from Peru's Ministerio de Cultura transparency portal:
50	  https://transparencia.cultura.gob.pe/sites/default/files/transparencia/2025/10/resoluciones-ministeriales/resolucionministerial-000284-2025-mcyanexo.pdf
51	  (Resolución Ministerial N° 000284-2025-MC, signed 30 Oct 2025 — promotional 2026 tariffs for
52	  national/resident/Andean-Community visitors to the Llaqta de Machupicchu; NOTE: these
53	  promotional soles rates do NOT apply to a foreign non-resident tourist like Elena, whose
54	  standard adult foreign-tourist rate is a separate ~S/152 (~US$40), rising to ~S/163 after
55	  1 May 2026 — see https://tuboleto.cultura.pe)
56	
57	- **`trip-budget.xlsx`** — built with `openpyxl`; every cost line is grounded in the same
58	  research pass:
59	  - Machu Picchu foreign-adult entrance ticket (~US$40 standard circuit): https://tuboleto.cultura.pe
60	  - Todo Turismo La Paz→Uyuni overnight bus (~US$43 one-way): https://todoturismo.bo/en/page-inicial-en-eng/
61	  - Torres del Paine high-season foreign entrance fee (~US$35): https://torresdelpaine.com/en/torres-del-paine-2/useful-tips/entrance-fees/
62	  - Cartagena old-town hostel dorm price range (~US$18–25/night): https://www.hostelworld.com/hostels/south-america/colombia/cartagena/
63	  - Brazil e-visa fee (~US$80.90): https://www.visahq.com/brazil/
64	  - ADO bus fares, LA2232 / AV9788 flights: same sources as `trip-notes.md` above
65	
66	- **`voice-memo.mp3` + `voice-memo.txt`** — generated with Azure OpenAI TTS
67	  (`https://lmthing-resource.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/tts/audio/speech`, voice
68	  `alloy`) from a Spanish script written for this fixture, and round-trip-verified by
69	  transcribing the produced MP3 back through the Whisper deployment on the same resource
70	  (`.../deployments/whisper/audio/transcriptions`). The script's one real-world anchor — the
71	  Cerro Churuquella viewpoint above Sucre — is grounded here:
72	  https://sucre.bo/las-12-estaciones-del-cerro-churuquella/ ,
73	  https://correodelsur.com/local/20250806/el-monumento-al-sagrado-corazon-en-el-cerro-churuquella-cumple-100-anos.html
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Unique tokens (one per fixture — an assertion can only use these)
78	
79	Verified disjoint by grepping the content of every fixture in this directory (`grep -rn` for
80	text files; `pdftotext`/`unzip -p`/`strings` extraction for the PDF, xlsx, and binary media;
81	`find`/`ls` for the two binary files whose distinguishing token lives in the filename, since
82	they carry no extractable text of their own). Each token below was found in exactly the one
83	fixture listed, and in no other fixture file in this directory (this `links.md` file itself is

commands.name: claude-hud:setup

commands.name: claude-hud:configure
commands.description: (claude-hud) Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
